description_text: Este é um  Banco de Talentos,  destinado ao cadastro de  Pessoas com Deficiência para todas as unidades da John Deere no Brasil . Se você é uma pessoa com deficiência, fique à vontade para se candidatar a qualquer outra posição disponível em nosso site. Caso não encontre nenhuma vaga que seja de seu interesse, cadastre-se aqui, e avaliaremos suas informações para futuras oportunidades que possam surgir. Seja você, seja John Deere. Faça parte do nosso time! Áreas para as quais recrutamos no Brasil Administração Geral; Contábil; Controladoria; Engenharia; Financeiro; Jurídico; Logística; Marketing; Operações; Order Fulfillment; Qualidade; Recursos Humanos; Saúde, Segurança e Meio Ambiente; Suporte ao produto; Suprimentos; Tecnologia da informação. Unidades John Deere Brasil: São Paulo Campinas Indaiatuba Goiás Catalão Rio Grande do Sul Horizontina Montenegro Quem não é uma pessoa com deficiência, pode se inscrever? Este banco de talentos é destinado a todas as pessoas com deficiência, caso não faça parte desse grupo, acesse nossas oportunidades na  página de carreiras . (Por favor, anexe o seu laudo) Por que a John Deere está fazendo um banco de talentos para pessoas com deficiências? Reconhecemos e valorizamos a pluralidade dos nossos times. Por isso, criamos condições para a equidade de oportunidades através de políticas, práticas e atitudes que promovam uma cultura de respeito e o desenvolvimento de talentos. O Que Você Receberá Na John Deere, você conseguirá desenvolver uma carreira que levará você aonde quer chegar. Além de produzirmos máquinas conectadas e inteligentes, com a mais alta qualidade, criamos soluções e serviços que garantam que nossos clientes possam gerir seus negócios de forma sustentável e produtiva. Com conexões tecnológicas, sociais e humanas, você terá liberdade de explorar novos projetos e pensar de forma inovadora.

Trabalhamos para que a vida possa avançar e estamos sempre à procura de pessoas inovadoras que possam contribuir com a nossa missão. Que tal fazer parte do nosso time? Local Principal: Brasil (BR) - São Paulo - Indaiatuba Função: Product Engineering (CA) Título: Pessoa Engenheira do Produto PL - Indaiatuba/SP - 118728 Suas Responsabilidades Como Pessoa Engenheira do Produto PL, no Centro de Pesquisa e Desenvolvimento da John Deere em Indaiatuba/SP, você atuará no desenvolvimento e melhoria contínua de componentes e sistemas para pulverizadores e plantadeiras, contribuindo diretamente para a evolução tecnológica das soluções agrícolas da John Deere. Principais Responsabilidades: Projetar novos componentes e sistemas ou aprimorar itens existentes para equipamentos agrícolas; Desenvolver e validar soluções envolvendo estruturas, hidráulica, elétrica e conjuntos mecânicos; Criar e atualizar desenhos técnicos, especificações e documentação de engenharia; Conduzir análises técnicas, revisões de design e investigações de falhas; Suportar todo o ciclo de desenvolvimento do produto, desde conceito até validação e produção; Colaborar com equipes multifuncionais (qualidade, manufatura, supply chain, validação etc.); Acompanhar feedback de campo, analisando causas e implementando melhorias; Garantir que as soluções atendam targets de performance, durabilidade, segurança e custo. Habilidades Que Você Precisa Graduação Completa em Engenharia Mecânica, Elétrica, Mecatrônica ou áreas correlatas; Experiência prévia em desenvolvimento de produtos, projetos mecânicos e de sistemas; Conhecimento e experiência com os softwares CREO e Ansys, ou ferramentas similares; Inglês avançado para comunicação com times globais e documentação técnica; Conhecimento em desenvolvimento de sistemas complexos (hidráulicos, elétricos, estruturais); Capacidade de interpretar requisitos, transformar necessidades em soluções e documentar projetos; Boa comunicação, visão colaborativa e habilidade de atuar em times multidisciplinares. O Que Faz Destacar Experiência com máquinas agrícolas, especialmente pulverizadores ou plantadeiras; Vivência com validação, testes e processos de ciclo de vida de produto; Experiência em suporte à manufatura ou a campo; Conhecimento em DFMEA, PFMEA e metodologias de melhoria contínua; Familiaridade com tecnologias de agricultura de precisão: spot spray, taxa variável, section control, JDLink e telemetria; Disponibilidade para viagens (até 25%). Nossos principais benefícios Remuneração variável; Plano de Saúde; Plano Odontológico; Seguro de Vida; Previdência Privada; Benefício Ótica; Reembolso vacina; Universidade Corporativa; TotalPass; Dependendo da unidade, você terá restaurante no local, vale refeição ou alimentação; Auxílio farmácia; Entre outros LI-Hybrid Nosso Processo Seletivo A seleção começa com a sua inscrição, para algumas vagas, existem alguns testes adicionais. Você terá a possibilidade de conhecer pessoas do time nas entrevistas! Nosso processo seletivo é flexível em termos de tempo, então não se preocupe! Nossa equipe de Talent Acquisition está comprometida em proporcionar a melhor experiência para você. Analisamos todas as candidaturas cuidadosamente e fornecemos feedback em todas as etapas do processo. Além disso, todas as orientações serão enviadas por e-mail! Sobre a John Deere A John Deere é líder global no fornecimento de produtos, tecnologia e serviços avançados para clientes que desempenham um papel essencial na agricultura, construção, florestal entre outros setores. Nosso objetivo é atender à crescente necessidade mundial de alimentos, combustível, abrigo e infraestrutura, enquanto promove a sustentabilidade e a preservação do planeta que chamamos de lar. Inovamos para impulsionar a produtividade e proteger nosso planeta e convidamos você a fazer parte dessa história. Trabalharemos juntos para criar, testar, superar para que para que a vida possa avançar. ??

Develop, code, configure, and test software programs and systems; Evaluate and implement design enhancements to improve the cost-effectiveness, quality, and performance of software applications; Analyze assignments and define technical specifications required to achieve project objectives; Produce and maintain comprehensive documentation as directed or required by the project scope; Collaborate with stakeholders and cross-functional teams to ensure that features align with business requirements; Follow industry-standard Agile methodologies for software design, development, and documentation; Mentor junior software engineers on the team to support professional growth and code quality. Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or equivalent fields; Advanced English (broad vocabulary, strong grammar, able to lead meetings, argue, negotiate, and conduct training sessions); Solid experience in development and implementation using ABAP, SAP Fiori technology, and frameworks; Experience in SAP SD and pricing configurations; Understanding of application development environments, database design, data management, and infrastructure capabilities/constraints; Ability to apply technical information to solve problems and conduct research; Understanding of security vulnerabilities and their remediation; Strong analytical, interpersonal communication, and collaboration skills, with experience in Agile development methodologies; Availability to work in a hybrid model, 3 times a week, in Indaiatuba/SP. Experience working with BADIs and User Exits; Experience providing technical support to end-users.

Trabalhamos para que a vida possa avançar e estamos sempre à procura de pessoas inovadoras que possam contribuir com a nossa missão. Que tal fazer parte do nosso time? Local Principal: Brasil (BR) - São Paulo - Indaiatuba Função: People and Culture (CA) Título: Vaga Afirmativa PCD - Pessoa Analista de Recursos Humanos PL - Indaiatuba/SP - 118287 Como Analista de Recursos Humanos Pleno na John Deere, alocado(a) na unidade de Florestal em Indaiatuba você será responsável pelo processo de onboarding e offboarding, suporte nos processos de recrutamento e seleção, gestão do programa de aprendizes e estagiários, comunicação interna, organização de eventos, gestão de benefícios como fretado, vale alimentação e outros, controle de jornada, NFs e rateios, interface com fornecedores, elaboração de relatórios para suporte à tomada de decisão, treinamentos e atendimento a funcionários contribuindo ativamente e agregando ao setor de Recursos Humanos. Principais Responsabilidades: Onboarding e offboarding Gestão de benefícios locais Desenvolvimento do programa de treinamento Comunicação interna e engajamento Administração de pessoal Habilidades Que Você Precisa: Graduação completa em Recursos Humanos, Administração ou Psicologia Experiência mínima de 2 anos em Recursos Humanos Inglês básico Conhecimento em aministração de pessoal, onboarding e offboarding O Que Faz Destacar: Pós-graduação Inglês avançado Habilidades com power BI Metodologias ágeis Nossos principais benefícios Remuneração variável; Plano de Saúde; Plano Odontológico; Seguro de Vida; Previdência Privada; Benefício Ótica; Reembolso vacina; Universidade Corporativa; TotalPass; Vale refeição ou alimentação; Auxílio farmácia; Entre outros Nosso Processo Seletivo A seleção começa com a sua inscrição, para algumas vagas, existem alguns testes adicionais. Você terá a possibilidade de conhecer pessoas do time nas entrevistas! Nosso processo seletivo é flexível em termos de tempo, então não se preocupe! Nossa equipe de Talent Acquisition está comprometida em proporcionar a melhor experiência para você. Analisamos todas as candidaturas cuidadosamente e fornecemos feedback em todas as etapas do processo. Além disso, todas as orientações serão enviadas por e-mail! Sobre a John Deere A John Deere é líder global no fornecimento de produtos, tecnologia e serviços avançados para clientes que desempenham um papel essencial na agricultura, construção, florestal entre outros setores. Nosso objetivo é atender à crescente necessidade mundial de alimentos, combustível, abrigo e infraestrutura, enquanto promove a sustentabilidade e a preservação do planeta que chamamos de lar. Inovamos para impulsionar a produtividade e proteger nosso planeta e convidamos você a fazer parte dessa história. Trabalharemos juntos para criar, testar, superar para que para que a vida possa avançar. ??

Build and validate predictive and behavioral models using techniques such as regression, classification, clustering, and time-series forecasting to support strategic decision-making; Design and deploy customer-facing AI solutions using Python, object-oriented programming, cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure), and data processing frameworks like DataBricks, PySpark, SQL; Apply statistical analysis, feature engineering, creating embedding models, and unsupervised learning to identify patterns and trends in structured and unstructured datasets using tools like SQL, Pandas, and Scikit-learn; Support design and management of pilot projects and proofs-of-concept, establishing success metrics and guiding teams through iterative testing and evaluation cycles; Work with engineers, researchers, and product managers to define early-stage use cases, document technical requirements, and prototype solutions using Jupyter notebooks or low-code platform; Oversee data ingestion pipelines, ensure schema consistency, and implement validation checks using ETL tools (e.g., Airflow, dbt) and data quality frameworks; Develop dashboards and reporting mechanisms using tools like Power BI, Tableau, or Looker to track project status and share insights with technical and non-technical audiences; Stay informed on advancements in AI, ML, and data engineering by attending conferences, reading academic papers, and experimenting with new tools and frameworks. Experience technical experience in product, innovation, or program management role; Experience providing technical leadership on a project Experience with data and using data in digital solutions to meaningfully collaborate with technical experts; Demonstrated ability to work with multiple cross-functional initiatives simultaneously; Highly motivated to work in a complex environment and demystifying emerging technology concepts for others. Experience in current and emerging AI practices (especially GenAI); Experience in early-stage product, R&D, innovation, or research organization roles is a plus.

Define and manage the go-to-market strategy for our fixed and mobile RFID readers and the Mobile Computing software portfolio. You will own the product positioning, pricing, and promotion to maximise market impact. Develop and execute strategic channel programs and initiatives to accelerate sales through our Direct, Partner, and Distributor networks. Your primary goal is to drive revenue attainment and ensure the successful adoption of new products. Act as the primary representative for the EMEA region, gathering market intelligence and customer feedback to influence the global product roadmap. You will own the EMEA input into our Product Advisory Committee (PAC) process. Become the go-to expert for our products. You will create and deliver compelling presentations to diverse audiences, from intimate partner meetings to large industry conferences, clearly articulating our value proposition and competitive advantages. Continuously analyse market trends, the competitive landscape, and customer needs to identify new opportunities and ensure our products are perfectly positioned to win. Marketing, Business Administration or Engineering degree, or equivalent experience. A strong background in Product Management or Product Marketing within the technology sector, with a solid understanding of channel sales models. A strong aptitude for understanding technology concepts, particularly related to RFID, Mobile Device Software, or Android OS, and the ability to translate technical features into clear business benefits. The ability to analyse market data and translate insights into actionable product and go-to-market requirements. Proficiency with Excel and PowerPoint is essential. Outstanding written and verbal communication skills, with the confidence to present to diverse audiences at all levels. Fluency in English is required, and an additional European language is a significant plus.

Operate and maintain supplies manufacturing machines and equipment while adhering to all quality and safety standards.Wristband cartridge assembler working on a 4 person production line. Performs packing, wristband, or pre-press functions and operates associated machinery Follows work order instructions and performs labeling requirement Performs quality tests per ISO procedures Complies with all required safety practices and use of PPE Resolves a variety of simple problems of limited complexity by following highly detailed instructions or procedures Receives direct supervision for routine work, new projects or assignments. Very limited requirement for decision making role. Any decisions have limited impact on team effectiveness. Work reviewed for accuracy and overall adequacy Able to work effectively with others and adhere to Zebra Leadership Behaviors. Accepts direction and instructions from supervisor High School or equivalent Ability to follow written and verbal instructions Learns policies and procedures and how to apply them to issues experienced in routine activities

Performs setup, testing and modular level repair to whole units. Maintains accurate record keeping for individual repairs. Uses custom software testing suites to diagnose/validate hardware failure. Recognizes failures, follows engineering changes, and determines appropriate action. Uses equipment such as a voltmeter, computer, and special test fixtures. Maintains production and quality output standards. Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) Required (safety glasses, gloves, etc.) Zebra provided High School Diploma or GED Experience as a production assembler, having performed assembly operations to required quality levels and job standards Manual dexterity and visual coordination are required. Prior use of various hand tools. Ability to stand and sit on production line throughout the shift. Works well with others. Work for a company that cares, offers a growth environment, and a culture of respect, inclusion and recognition - our front-line workers have an average 7-year tenure! Healthcare, Dental & Vision, Tuition Reimbursement 401k with Company Match Quarterly Cash Incentive Opportunity 20 Days Starting PTO 2 Floating Holidays and Paid Volunteer Time Off Annual Shoe Allowance and free Company Apparel

Working with Account or Channel Management, owns development and execution of technology strategy. Designs solutions that meet customer needs and beat competition. Develops strong relationships with sales team, internal engineering, and key technical customer contacts and leverages to Zebra's advantage; creates and delivers high impact solution presentations. Business and Financial Impact - Responsible for individual revenue attainment with established prices and personal expenses Relative Size and Scope - Moderate to average individual quota size for business and like roles Types of Projects - Many transactions with some moderately complex deals Strategic Impact for Zebra - Medium/mid-term Technical Skills - Uses moderate domain/solutions knowledge Knowledge of Zebra - Utilizes solid understanding of all Products/Services in business Sales Skills - Applies Solution Selling concepts to customer situations; excellent communication and interpersonal skills Managerial Skills - Understands policies and practices related to role and shares ideas for improvement Business Acumen - Understands and explains how Zebra solutions can help customer's business results; obtains profitable revenue growth via value proposition vs. competition Market/customer Knowledge - Uses full knowledge of customer's business and market economics/trends to position effectively versus competition Engineering degree or equivalent experience preferred 4-5 years of relevant work experience Uses moderate domain/solutions knowledge Applies Solution Selling concepts to customer situations; excellent communication and interpersonal skills Understands policies and practices related to role and shares ideas for improvement Higher level technical skills than typical sales criteria Mastery of the Zebra Portfolio: Mobile Computing: In-depth knowledge of Zebra's mobile computers (e.g., TC and MC series), their differences, use cases, and the Android Enterprise operating system. Scanning and Data Capture: Expertise in barcode technologies (1D/2D), RFID, and how they are applied in different industries. Printing: Understanding of Zebra's printers (desktop, industrial, mobile) and consumables (labels, ribbons). Software and Analytics: Familiarity with Zebra's software ecosystem (Zebra DNA Cloud, Workforce Connect) and how it integrates to provide additional value. Networking: Solid knowledge of Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and cellular networks to ensure devices operate optimally in complex customer environments (warehouses, retail stores, hospitals). Systems Integration: Understanding of how Zebra solutions integrate with key enterprise systems like ERP (SAP, Oracle), WMS (Manhattan, Blue Yonder), and Point of Sale (POS) systems. API knowledge is a plus. These are crucial for being the technical face of the company to the customer. Consultative Selling: The ability to listen to the customer, understand their business problems (not just their technical requirements), and design a solution that solves those problems. Exceptional Communication: Presentations and Demos: The ability to deliver compelling and customized product demonstrations that show how the solution solves the customer's specific problems. Technical Translation: The ability to explain complex technical concepts to both technical audiences (e.g., an IT Director) and non-technical audiences (e.g., an Operations Manager). Problem-Solving: Being creative and analytical to overcome technical objections and find solutions to challenges that arise during the sales cycle. Cross-functional Collaboration: Working smoothly with sales, product, engineering, and post-sales support teams to ensure a consistent customer experience. Self-Management and Proactivity: The ability to manage their own time, prioritize opportunities, and continuously learn about new technologies and products.

Remote Work: Hybrid Overview: At Zebra, we are a community of innovators who come together to create new ways of working. United by curiosity and a culture of caring, we develop smart solutions that anticipate our customer's and partner's needs and solve their challenges. Being a part of Zebra Nation means you are seen, heard, valued, and respected. Drawing from our unique perspectives, we collaborate to deliver on our purpose. Here you are a part of a team pushing boundaries today to redefine the work of tomorrow for organizations, their employees, and those they serve. You'll have opportunities to learn and lead in a forward-thinking environment, defining your path to a fulfilling career while channeling your skills toward causes you care about - locally and globally. Come make an impact every day at Zebra. This role will contribute to the architecting, evaluation, improvement, and support of system solutions that utilize the organization's application engineering portfolio. The goal of this job is to ensure that customer requirements can be successfully met or exceeded. This person helps develop, validate, test, and integrate software and hardware products in the pursuit of cost-effective and market-ready solutions to customer challenges. This person contributes to technical analyses, planning, and design efforts that may identify products/solutions for roadmap consideration. Responsibilities: Essential Duties and Responsibilities: Serve as a technical resource, addressing key challenges related to the performance of Zebra's products and solutions in specific application scenarios. Implement testing and troubleshooting strategies. Analyze key test data to identify issues and propose potential solutions. Collaborates with more senior AEs, as well as sales, engineering, and product management teams to identify and execute resolution. Provide analysis and input on how products/solutions can best meet or exceed customer/partner business requirements, as well as on how they perform/compared to competitive options Provides input to the analysis of strategic customer projects to ensure that they are effectively executed. With guidance from senior colleagues, engage in software design, hardware analysis, integration work, and/or solution development for proof of concepts. Represent the product management organization as a technical customer/partner contact; often assist with application deployments with the guidance of more senior colleagues For high priority opportunities, may be asked to assist sales team/partners with deployment and/or testing on site at customer locations. May be asked to engage with product management colleagues to communicate market needs and inform and influence some aspects of the product portfolio and roadmap May be asked to assist with the development of technical training and/or solution documentation Performs benchmarking to understand the competitive marketplace. Provides critical technical product feedback to engineering, sales, and product teams Qualifications: Minimum Requirements: o Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Electrical engineering or in a related field is required o 2+ years of experience working with advanced data capture and retail point of sale solutions. o Knowledge of basic electrical engineering concepts like schematics, wiring, hand tools,etc. o Knowledge of basic imaging concepts. Preferred Requirements: o Knowledge of retail point of sale processes and environments o Knowledge of - and experience with - coding languages like Windows, Linux, Python, etc o Travel will be 30% or more. Zebra is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace All qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identify, national origin, disability and protected veteran status or any other basis prohibited by law. If you are an individual with a disability and need assistance in applying for a position, please contact us at workplace.accommodations@zebra.com. Know Your Rights: https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2022-10/EEOC_KnowYourRights_screen_reader_10_20.pdf Conozca sus Derechos: https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2022-10/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRightsSp_10_20.pdf We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation. Zebra is a federal contractor and is committed to an alcohol and drug free workplace. As a result, all U.S. based employees are subject to the Drug and Alcohol Free Workplace Policy and Procedure. To all recruitment agencies: Zebra does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to Zebra employees or any other team members. Zebra is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes and direct engagement with Zebra employees. Zebra Total Rewards includes more than just pay and is structured to meet the needs of our changing global business and evolving talent. We are committed to providing our employees with a benefits program that is comprehensive and competitive - including healthcare, wellness, inclusion networks, and continued learning and development offerings. We offer community service days, in addition to the traditional insurances, compensation, parental leave, employee assistance program and paid time off offerings depending on the country where you work. Salary:  USD 110000.00 Yearly Salary offered will vary depending on your location, job-related skills, knowledge, and experience. Additionally, all Zebra roles are eligible for cash incentive programs. For example, sales roles have additional opportunity to earn substantial variable compensation tied to quota achievement. In most other roles, the Zebra annual cash incentive program links Company and individual performance together. Some roles may also be eligible for long-term incentive equity awards. To protect candidates from falling victim to online fraudulent activity involving fake job postings and employment offers, please be aware our recruiters will always connect with you via @zebra.com email accounts. Applications are only accepted through our applicant tracking system and only accept personal identifying information through that system. Our Talent Acquisition team will not ask for you to provide personal identifying information via e-mail or outside of the system. If you are a victim of identity theft contact your local police department. Zebra Technologies leverages AI technology to evaluate job applications using objective, job-relevant criteria. This approach enhances efficiency and promotes fairness in the hiring process. However, every decision regarding interviews and hiring is made by our dedicated team, because we believe people make the best decisions about people. For more on how we use technology in hiring and how we process applicant data, see our Zebra Privacy Policy.

Performs the physical and/or administrative tasks involved in the shipping, receiving, storing, distributing and order fulfillment of merchandise, products, materials, parts, supplies and equipment. Performs quality checks on outbound and inbound shipments. Performs outbound staging and packing in accordance with site policies. Executes ERP system transactions on outbound and inbound shipments. Updates FMS for parcel and freight shipments. Learns to use handheld RF scanners and Warehouse Management System to fulfill orders. Maintains inventory accuracy. Handles and controls hazardous material on both inbound and outbound. Complies with 5S safety, housekeeping practices, and regulations. Completes all required safety training and follows protocol in all risk situations. Uses Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) appropriate to tasks at hand. Lifts up to 75 pounds unassisted. Certification on powered industrial trucks including work assist vehicle (WAV), off the ground order picker (up to 35' off the ground), reach truck, dock stocker and powered pallet jacks. Creates repair RMA's, manages receiving and shipment issues within repair systems. Develops familiarity with a subset of products, processes, operational metrics and software tools; learns and follows basic work instructions for functional assignments according to productivity and quality standards Provides input geared towards continuous improvement, work instruction documentation, and standardization of processes. Resolves a variety of basic problems of limited complexity under direct supervision and performs rudimentary analysis involving a limited number of factors. Creates expense orders for internal repair consumption. Full knowledge of ISO requirements and demonstrated ability to ensure compliance with external auditors. High School Diploma or GED Basic computer literacy, with knowledge of Microsoft Office. Proficient understanding of the use of a desktop. Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) Required (safety glasses, steel-toed boots, gloves, etc.) Working from heights such as roofs, ladders, or powered lifts. Moving self in different positions to accomplish tasks in various environments including awkward or tight and confined spaces. Remaining in a stationary position, often standing or sitting for prolonged periods. Stooping, kneeling, bending, crouching, reaching, pushing/pulling. Moving about to accomplish tasks or moving from one worksite to another. Adjusting or moving objects up to 75 pounds in all directions. Communicating with others to exchange information. Repeating motions that may include the wrists, hands and/or fingers. Operating motor vehicles, industrial vehicles, or heavy equipment. Assessing the accuracy, neatness and thoroughness of the work assigned. Come join the herd! Work for a company that cares, offers a growth environment, and a culture of respect, inclusion and recognition - our front-line workers have an average 7-year tenure! Healthcare, Dental & Vision, Tuition Reimbursement 401k with Company Match Quarterly Cash Incentive Opportunity 20 Days Starting PTO 2 Floating Holidays and Paid Volunteer Time Off Annual Shoe Allowance and free Company Apparel

Develop and execute winning technology strategies in collaboration with our Inside Channel Account Managers (ICAMs) team. Design and architect creative solutions that directly address the needs of partners and their customers to give us a competitive edge. Build and maintain strong, collaborative relationships with our internal ICAMs and the key technical contacts at the partner accounts they manage. Create and deliver compelling, high-impact solution presentations and product demonstrations that captivate both technical and business audiences. Act as the primary technical expert, solving partner problems and verifying solution compatibility to ensure success. Clearly articulate the business value of Zebra's solutions, showing partners how our technology can drive their business results and fuel profitable growth. A Bachelor's degree in Engineering (or equivalent experience) paired with 2-5 years of relevant work in a technical sales or solutions role. Fluent in English and German is mandatory A strong technical aptitude with the ability to quickly master a diverse portfolio of products and solutions. Proven success in applying solution-selling concepts to understand customer needs and craft tailored proposals. Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with a natural ability to explain complex technical ideas in a simple, persuasive way. Sharp business acumen and an understanding of market trends, with the ability to connect our solutions to a customer's bottom line. A proactive and independent mindset, with the judgment to manage complex projects and make sound decisions as a key member of our team.

Remote Work: Hybrid Overview: At Zebra, we are a community of innovators who come together to create new ways of working. United by curiosity and a culture of caring, we develop smart solutions that anticipate our customer's and partner's needs and solve their challenges. Being a part of Zebra Nation means you are seen, heard, valued, and respected. Drawing from our unique perspectives, we collaborate to deliver on our purpose. Here you are a part of a team pushing boundaries today to redefine the work of tomorrow for organizations, their employees, and those they serve. You'll have opportunities to learn and lead in a forward-thinking environment, defining your path to a fulfilling career while channeling your skills toward causes you care about - locally and globally. Come make an impact every day at Zebra. The Order Experience (OEX) organization is responsible for all aspects of customer care related to the quote-to-order process, including people, process, tools and customer satisfaction. The team is responsible for the accurate entry of purchase order and/or Software information from the sales team or directly from Zebra's customers into Zebra's system. They act as a subject matter expert on all matters related to the product and/or service contract orders, responding to inquiries from management, Zebra's sales teams and the customer. Good opportunity for russian speaking candidates! Position: Brno Responsibilities: Learns and applies knowledge of Zebra's systems, processes, and philosophies around management of product and/or service contract orders. Enters new order and/or service contract data into the system, ensuring data accuracy and process adherence. Meets or exceeds all departmental or individual metrics associated with performance against service level agreements Provides price quotes on request. Ensures proper recording and tracking of all assigned orders Responds to customer or internal inquiries regarding order status, invoices, service contract entitlements etc. Escalates any new or complex problems to management for assistance Coordinates with other internal departments to drive issue resolution regarding order status, invoicing, shipping dates, product availability, discounts, quality issues, damaged or mis-shipped product, resolution of other issues etc. Adheres to OEX customer strategy, leads by example and follows our customer principles. Delivers a positive ordering and invoicing experience to our customers and partners. Qualifications: High School or equivalent Strong data entry skills with attention to detail and quality. Strong process adherence. Fluent level of English and Russian (written and verbal) as well as local language as applicable. Beginner level skills in Microsoft business applications including Word, Excel and Outlook Customer service values / orientation. Solid professional work behaviors (attendance, teamwork, time management). Strong communication skills (listening, providing clear and concise information, using proper language and communication channels Benefits: 5 weeks of vacation, 5 sick days and 4 volunteer days paid off. Multisport card at a reduced price. Meal contribution. Monthly contribution 1000 CZK/month in the form of credits on your Benefit plus card with cafeteria system where you can choose from wide range of activities/benefits from Health, Sports, Travel, Wellness, Development, Culture up to company Language courses. Training and personal development in soft skills and hard skills, access to our internal learning portal and internal career opportunities within Zebra departments. Annual bonuses based on financial results. Yearly salary increases according to individual performance. Employee referral bonus for bringing New Talent to Zebra. Works with latest technologies and new approaches Reviews changes or upgrades to existing designs or test Exercises judgment in selecting methods and techniques for obtaining solutions on assignments Electrical Engineering BS degree. Read technical documents and report creation. Make Power Point presentation of findings. Microsoft Office suite skills Communication skills - able to clearly define problem, ideas or solutions. Develops, designs, evaluates, and/or modifies systems consistent with customer and regulatory requirements. Develops system architecture, and ensures the integrity of the architecture/design. Performs system feasibility studies and evaluates alternative technology applications. Collaborates with Product Management and Customers to decompose customer use cases into correct features for new product development. Defines the solutions and system requirements for highly complex system design projects. Represents systems engineering as technical contact on assigned projects. Successfully collaborates and communicates with software and hardware sub-system development teams. Integrates new technologies and solutions based on emerging market/industry technologies. Assimilates technology roadmaps in critical product areas. Lead product feature trade-offs for performance, cost and schedule, while meeting the customer expectations Utilizes, develops, and applies advanced tools, including AI-powered solutions, for requirements analysis, system design optimization, automated validation, and the resolution of complex problems. Evaluates competitor products through data driven competitive benchmarking. Manages and coordinates the activities of other engineering personnel and often performs technical project leadership. Mentors more junior level personnel. Work in the agile development process to release products in a timely fashion Exercises independent judgment in methods, techniques and evaluation criteria for obtaining results. Networks with key contacts outside their own area of expertise. Interacts with Product Management to provide technical responses to RFPs and RFIs which lead to successful future engagement with key customers. Will be responsible for supporting lighthouse customer opportunities and accounts via the customer engagement methodology. Bachelor's degree in engineering with 5 + years of experience or Masters degree and 4+ year experience Proven System Engineering experience ideally in the Mobile computing space Able to clearly communicate complex concepts to broadly diverse audiences and guide both local and remote teams in achieving their goals. Experienced in defining system requirements from complex customer use cases. Demonstrated proficiency in dealing with ambiguity and ability to stay focused and to clear barriers. Strong ability to present detailed information at the appropriate level. Experience working on Handheld devices on Android platform. Successfully held a recent role as a System Engineering role working on a product development effort that ultimately led to a successful Market launch/customer delivery. Recent hands-on experience in the full lifecycle (cradle to grave) development of Enterprise class productivity devices and/or the associated application ecosystem is highly desirable. Working knowledge of the Agile development process. Experience in hardware design and implementation. Experience in solutions for Payment/POS applications is a plus. Experience in Android HAL, Framework, Security, and Applications. Recent working knowledge of latest wireless technologies spanning: (i.e.) 4G/LTE/5G NR, GNSS/UWB, Wi-Fi/802.11(x), NFC, HF/UHF-RFID, Bluetooth/BLE.

Develops and executes sales strategy for assigned account(s) or territory with manager's input; develops strong relationships with key customer contacts and leverages to Zebra's advantage; creates and delivers high impact sales presentations and capable of product training; often used for those with strong technical background but limited sales experience. Business and Financial Impact - Responsible for individual revenue attainment with established prices and personal expenses Relative Size and Scope - Moderate to average individual quota size for business and like roles Types of Projects - Many transactions with some moderately complex deals Strategic Impact for Zebra - Medium/mid-term Technical Skills - Uses moderate domain/solutions knowledge Knowledge of Zebra - Utilizes solid understanding of all Products/Services in business Sales Skills - Applies Solution Selling concepts to customer situations; excellent communication and interpersonal skills Managerial Skills - Understands policies and practices related to role and shares ideas for improvement Business Acumen - Understands and explains how Zebra solutions can help customer's business results; obtains profitable revenue growth via value proposition vs. competition Market/customer Knowledge - Uses full knowledge of customer's business and market economics/trends to position effectively versus competition Bachelors or equivalent experience 2-5 years of applicable work experience Uses moderate domain/solutions knowledge Applies Solution Selling concepts to customer situations; excellent communication and interpersonal skills Understands policies and practices related to role and shares ideas for improvement Solution-Oriented Approach: The ability to propose advanced purchasing models like blanket orders or the Supplies Management Program. Long-Term Relationship Building: Creating "stickiness" through convenience and security, making price a secondary factor. Proactive Thinking: Anticipating customer needs so they never run out of stock, ensuring a recurring and predictable revenue stream. Active Listening: Using conversations about supplies to detect signals of need for new hardware (obsolete printers, manual processes, etc.). Business Acumen: Understanding that their role is to connect the customer to Zebra's entire ecosystem, not just sell their product. Communication with Other Teams: The ability to pass qualified hardware leads to the appropriate sales team. Autonomy and Curiosity: The ability to self-educate using platforms like the Zebra Supplies Knowledge Platform and the Supplies eBook. Internal Collaboration: Knowing when and how to involve the Zebra Quoting Team to expedite complex quotes. Strategic Use of Incentives: Actively proposing the Free Custom Label Trial Fund as a tool to eliminate customer risk and close the sale

Remote Work: Hybrid Overview: At Zebra, we are a community of innovators who come together to create new ways of working. United by curiosity and a culture of caring, we develop smart solutions that anticipate our customer's and partner's needs and solve their challenges. Being a part of Zebra Nation means you are seen, heard, valued, and respected. Drawing from our unique perspectives, we collaborate to deliver on our purpose. Here you are a part of a team pushing boundaries today to redefine the work of tomorrow for organizations, their employees, and those they serve. You'll have opportunities to learn and lead in a forward-thinking environment, defining your path to a fulfilling career while channeling your skills toward causes you care about - locally and globally. Come make an impact every day at Zebra. The Order Experience (OEX) organization is responsible for all aspects of customer care related to the quote-to-order process, including people, process, tools and customer satisfaction. The team is responsible for the accurate entry of purchase order and/or Software information from the sales team or directly from Zebra's customers into Zebra's system. They act as a subject matter expert on all matters related to the product and/or service contract orders, responding to inquiries from management, Zebra's sales teams and the customer. Position: Brno Responsibilities: Learns and applies knowledge of Zebra's systems, processes, and philosophies around management of product and/or service contract orders. Enters new order and/or service contract data into the system, ensuring data accuracy and process adherence. Meets or exceeds all departmental or individual metrics associated with performance against service level agreements Provides price quotes on request. Ensures proper recording and tracking of all assigned orders Responds to customer or internal inquiries regarding order status, invoices, service contract entitlements etc. Escalates any new or complex problems to management for assistance Coordinates with other internal departments to drive issue resolution regarding order status, invoicing, shipping dates, product availability, discounts, quality issues, damaged or mis-shipped product, resolution of other issues etc. Adheres to OEX customer strategy, leads by example and follows our customer principles. Delivers a positive ordering and invoicing experience to our customers and partners. Qualifications: High School or equivalent Strong data entry skills with attention to detail and quality. Strong process adherence. Fluent level of English (written and verbal) as well as local language as applicable. Beginner level skills in Microsoft business applications including Word, Excel and Outlook Customer service values / orientation. Solid professional work behaviors (attendance, teamwork, time management). Strong communication skills (listening, providing clear and concise information, using proper language and communication channels Benefits: 5 weeks of vacation, 5 sick days and 4 volunteer days paid off. Multisport card at a reduced price. Meal contribution. Monthly contribution 1000 CZK/month in the form of credits on your Benefit plus card with cafeteria system where you can choose from wide range of activities/benefits from Health, Sports, Travel, Wellness, Development, Culture up to company Language courses. Training and personal development in soft skills and hard skills, access to our internal learning portal and internal career opportunities within Zebra departments. Annual bonuses based on financial results. Yearly salary increases according to individual performance. Employee referral bonus for bringing New Talent to Zebra. Free beverages and fruit days (coffee, tea, milk available in our kitchenette). Life & Pension insurance contribution. New modern and multifunctional workplace at Vlněna Office park in the city centre Discount at our partners (Banks, HP, Dell, Microsoft Office). Remote Work: Hybrid Overview: At Zebra, we are a community of innovators who come together to create new ways of working. United by curiosity and a culture of caring, we develop smart solutions that anticipate our customer's and partner's needs and solve their challenges. Being a part of Zebra Nation means you are seen, heard, valued, and respected. Drawing from our unique perspectives, we collaborate to deliver on our purpose. Here you are a part of a team pushing boundaries today to redefine the work of tomorrow for organizations, their employees, and those they serve. You'll have opportunities to learn and lead in a forward-thinking environment, defining your path to a fulfilling career while channeling your skills toward causes you care about - locally and globally. Come make an impact every day at Zebra. The Zebra Technologies Internship Program is designed to bridge the gap between academia and the professional world, equipping talented college students for a successful career in business. We provide a holistic experience that immerses you in our organization, allowing you to fully envision your future with Zebra. To position you for success, the program includes targeted training, exposure to diverse business functions, social networking events, a dedicated professional mentor, and development sessions designed to help you discover and sharpen your unique performance edge. In this specific role, you will take ownership of a critical initiative: creating the definitive playbook for onboarding new suppliers and offboarding existing ones. You will be responsible for detailing the complete process map, defining owners and lead times, mapping dependencies, and identifying key milestones and completion criteria. This project offers an unparalleled opportunity to collaborate with cross-functional partners and gain deep, practical insight into Zebra's internal operations. This role is expected to start in May and run through August. This posting is for an existing vacancy at Zebra Technologies. Responsibilities: Discovery & Analysis: Conduct stakeholder interviews to document the current state of supplier management, identifying existing processes, pain points, and critical risk areas. Benchmarking: Leverage market intelligence and industry best practices to benchmark and identify opportunities for improving Zebra's supplier onboarding and offboarding processes. Process Design & Optimization: Design and propose an optimized, end-to-end process flow, incorporating feedback from cross-functional stakeholders to gain final approval. Documentation & Governance: Create detailed process maps and a comprehensive RACI (Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed) chart to ensure clear ownership and accountability. Communication & Reporting: Establish a regular communication cadence with management, providing clear and concise readouts on project milestones, progress, and dependencies. Professional Development Opportunities Interpersonal & Networking Skills: Build and maintain strong relationships with key business contacts through effective communication and consistent follow-up activities. Project Management: Develop robust planning, organizational, and leadership skills by driving the project initiative from conception to completion. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Enhance team-based work proficiencies by navigating and collaborating with a diverse set of partners across the organization THIS IS A HYBRID INTERNSHIP. THIS INTERN MUST BE ABLE TO WORK ONSITE AT ZEBRA'S MISSISSAUGA, ONTARIO OFFICE AT LEAST 3 DAYS/WEEK. Qualifications: Minimum Qualifications: Current enrollment in a MBA with an anticipated graduation date by May 2027 Must be authorized to work in Canada without requiring sponsorship now or in the future Preferred Qualifications: A proactive and intellectually curious learner with a passion for personal growth. Strong business acumen with the ability to transform data into insights and strategic solutions. Proven leadership and a history of excellence in professional, academic, or other settings. Highly collaborative with demonstrated success working within a team environment. Excellent verbal and written communication skills. Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite and modern AI productivity tools. Rate: CAD $20.50 \- CAD $29.50 hourly Zebra is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identify, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other basis prohibited by law. Designs, develops, builds and tests systems and products based on optic, photonic and laser technologies. Support concept generation, design, testing and implementation for product development. Creates engineering documentation for design of optical instruments. Delivers support for manufacturing optical instruments and products.
